page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon processors running at 2.6 GHz, corresponding to 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The memory configuration provides 768 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Cache resources include 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and a 54 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The instance achieves top-tier results in cryptographic operations, specifically the PassMark CPU encryption test, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Multi-core processing, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall into the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with uncached read speeds reaching 333.34 GB/sec and write speeds at 144.43 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation are relatively limited, with the latter falling into the bottom 10% tier due to the lack of GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-highmem-96 offers a dense allocation of memory relative to compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. It is also well-suited for software compilation and medium-scale LLM prompt processing, though it is not recommended for heavy LLM text generation tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM, powered by 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processors with 48 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 333.34 GB/sec and top-tier cryptographic execution. While single-core speeds and large-scale LLM text generation are modest, its massive memory-to-vCPU ratio (8 GB per core) and high multi-threaded throughput make it highly efficient for memory-intensive applications. This server is best suited for large-scale database operations, Redis caching, software compilation, and enterprise web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highmem-96 features 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs, engineered for demanding database operations and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance and high-speed memory bandwidth
Top-tier cryptographic execution with hardware-accelerated instruction sets
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for large databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
